tool_input.summary: To evaluate the stratigraphic relationships among silica outcrops, Halley-class bedrock, and Home Plate volcaniclastic deposits (MER2/Spirit, Gusev crater), you’ll want (1) calibrated/derived imaging to map contacts and measure bedding/relative elevation, (2) Mini-TES spectra to identify silica vs basaltic/volcaniclastic units, and (3) APXS/MI/MB contact-science data to tie composition/texture to mapped units. Below are the most relevant PDS datasets on the PDS Geosciences Node that support that workflow (favoring PDS4 collection LIDs).

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset for stratigraphic mapping at Home Plate: radiometrically calibrated Pancam frames (multispectral) used to delineate contacts between Halley-class bedrock, volcaniclastic layers, and silica-rich outcrops/bright soils; also supports measuring layer attitudes and tracing units laterally in stereo/mosaics.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Derived Color Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Color mosaics provide the broader outcrop context needed to place silica patches relative to Home Plate’s volcaniclastic stratigraphy and adjacent Halley-class bedrock; helpful for correlating units across traverses.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection (derived IOF)
candidates.reasoning: IOF products (reflectance factor) are commonly used for spectral parameters/ratios to discriminate silica-rich materials and altered coatings from basaltic volcaniclastic deposits and Halley-class bedrock in a stratigraphic context.

candidates.title: MER2 Navcam Calibrated Radiance Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Navcam calibrated panoramas/frames provide geomorphic and topographic context (e.g., slopes, benches, relative positions around Home Plate) to support stratigraphic interpretations derived from Pancam and contact science.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Key for distinguishing silica-rich materials (spectral signatures) from unaltered/volcaniclastic units and for tying those compositions to specific stratigraphic horizons around Home Plate.

candidates.title: Mini-TES EDC Averaged Mirror-Dust Corrected Image Cube Collection
candidates.reasoning: Enhanced Mini-TES emissivity (mirror-dust corrected; PDART product) often used for improved compositional discrimination of silica vs basaltic materials; particularly useful for robustly mapping where silica occurs relative to Home Plate strata.

candidates.title: MER APXS Oxide Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Derived APXS oxide abundances let you directly compare silica enrichment and alteration chemistry among targets classified as Halley-class bedrock, Home Plate volcaniclastics, and silica-rich outcrops/soils; essential for linking stratigraphic position with geochemistry.

candidates.title: MER2 APXS Xray Spectra (Partially Processed) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: If you need to reproduce or refine oxide derivations/uncertainties for specific Home Plate targets, the partially processed APXS spectra provide the measurement-level basis for compositional comparisons across stratigraphic units.

candidates.title: MER2 Microscopic Imager Calibrated Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: MI images constrain textures (e.g., sinter-like nodules/coatings vs clastic volcaniclastic grains) and contact relationships at sub-mm scale, supporting stratigraphic placement of silica-rich materials relative to bedrock and tephra layers.

candidates.title: MER MB Relative Iron Abundance Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Mössbauer-derived Fe mineral/abundance context can help distinguish altered silica-rich materials from less-altered volcaniclastic units and Halley-class bedrock, complementing APXS and Mini-TES in stratigraphic interpretations.

candidates.title: MER Contact Science Target List Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides the authoritative list of contact-science targets/activities (APXS/MI/MB/RAT) used in Home Plate studies; helpful for pulling the exact observations associated with ‘Halley-class’ and silica targets and tying them to specific outcrops/stratigraphic positions.

candidates.title: MER APXS Composite Spectra Library
candidates.reasoning: A higher-level APXS product (composites + enriched metadata) that can speed up identifying and comparing compositions of silica vs Halley-class vs volcaniclastic targets, especially when aggregating many measurements.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX → mex/ (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO → mro/ (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL → msl/ (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 → m2020/ (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) → mer/ (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS → mgs/ (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY → ody/ (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX → phx/ (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 → viking/ (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS → messenger/ (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN → mgn/ (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry, emissivity)
  LRO → lro/ (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL → grail/ (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR → near/ (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: fields.VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID (or top-level DATA_SET_ID)
  - PDS4 bundle: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier
  - PDS4 collection: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools):
  1. pds_geo_list_missions() — returns all 24 mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP. Use this to identify which mission to explore.
  2. pds_geo_list_dataset_dirs(path) — lists sub-directory names under a mission (e.g. path='mex/'). Cheap HTTP, no label parsing. Each dir gets a pds_hint ('PDS3'/'PDS4'/null) from its naming convention. Use this to see what datasets exist, then pick which to probe.
  3. pds_geo_probe_datasets(paths) — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Recurses one level to find leaf nodes. Returns dataset_id, title, pds_version, and slimmed fields. Accepts a list of paths (max 20) so you can batch multiple probes in one call.
  4. pds_geo_inspect_collections(path) — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection*.xml labels. Returns collection-level logical_identifiers. Use ONLY after probe_datasets confirms a PDS4 bundle exists.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires you to return dataset candidates. Queries come from published scientific papers and always imply specific PDS datasets. NEVER dismiss a query as 'interpretation' or 'conceptual'. Always return at least one candidate.

  2. Prefer CALIBRATED and REDUCED data products over raw/EDR when the query implies scientific analysis. Scientists use calibrated (RDR, CDR) or derived (DDR) products. Look for directory names containing 'calibrated', 'rdr', 'cdr', 'ddr'.

  3. For PDS4 datasets, use inspect_collections ONLY for the top 2-3 most relevant bundles. Return the COLLECTION-level LID when a :data or :calibrated collection exists, not just the bundle LID.

  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.

WORKFLOW (follow this order):
  Step 1: Infer the mission(s) and instrument(s) from the query using the abbreviation table above.
  Step 2: Call list_dataset_dirs for the relevant mission directory (e.g. 'mex/'). Scan the directory names and pds_hints to identify the most promising dataset directories.
  Step 3: Call probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch them — up to 20 paths in one call). This returns dataset_id, title, and pds_version for each.
  Step 4: If any probed datasets are PDS4 bundles and you need collection-level LIDs, call inspect_collections on the top 2-3.
  Step 5: Return the candidates with dataset_id, title, and reasoning.

  Skip list_missions if you already know the mission directory from the abbreviation table. Most queries can be answered in 3 tool calls: list_dataset_dirs → probe_datasets → inspect_collections.

Constraints:
  - Stay under 8 tool calls per query. This is a hard limit.
  - Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  - You MUST return at least one candidate for every query.

Below are the most relevant PDS datasets on the PDS Geosciences Node that support that workflow (favoring PDS4 collection LIDs).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_pancam_sci_calibrated2:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-pancam-3-radcal-sci-v2/mer2pc_1002","title":"MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary dataset for stratigraphic mapping at Home Plate: radiometrically calibrated Pancam frames (multispectral) used to delineate contacts between Halley-class bedrock, volcaniclastic layers, and silica-rich outcrops/bright soils;
